title: DirectorySync
subtitle: Directory group synchronization tool for keeping folders aligned
description: A directory synchronization app focused on managing groups of folders and keeping each group aligned.
It allows users to create sync groups, add or remove paths, preview changes, and run safe multi-directory synchronization.
features: Add and remove directory paths from each group
Select a group and view the paths included in that group
Preview planned sync actions before applying changes
Synchronize all directories inside a selected group
Preserve conflicts instead of automatically choosing a newest file
Archive deleted files instead of permanently removing them by default
Use configurable filters to skip OS-managed folders and internal sync data
Ability to report errors encountered during use
Ability to submit suggestions and improvement ideas
statusDetails: Complete: Add and remove path workflow
Complete: Conflict and delete safety handling

downloadLinks.title: Product Listing
downloadLinks.body: Directory group sync utility designed to keep multiple folders aligned while preserving conflicts and deleted-file archives.

howItWorks: DirectorySync organizes folders into named sync groups.
Each group contains multiple paths that should share the same file contents.
The selected group is passed into DirSync for previewing, synchronization, filtering, conflict handling, and delete archive handling.
setupInstall: Add two or more folder paths to the group
Use Preview to review planned actions
apiStructure: The application UI manages groups and user actions.
Settings and app controls store groups, paths, modes, and filter options.

progressNotes: Defined the app as a DirSync-based directory group manager
Planned group selection, path listing, add path, remove path, preview, and sync workflows
Built the initial desktop application around OS_Tools\DirUtilitys\DirSync
Added group list behavior and path management
Connected selected groups to preview and sync actions
Added runtime icon loading for Windows and Linux
Prepared application icon paths for packaged builds
Created initial user manual, README, changelog, license, and EULA documents
Documented directory group workflow and DirSync backend usage

commonQuestions.question: What is a directory group?
commonQuestions.answer: A directory group is a named set of folders that DirectorySync keeps aligned with each other.

commonQuestions.question: Should I preview before syncing?
commonQuestions.answer: Yes. Preview mode lets you review planned actions before files are changed.

commonQuestions.question: What happens if two folders changed the same file?
commonQuestions.answer: DirectorySync preserves the conflicting versions in an in-place conflict folder for manual resolution.
